Authenticates an admin user and returns an access token.

This endpoint allows customers to sign in with their email and password.

See Response -> Data -> customer_details below for the customerID used in other requests.

id_token.description: JWT ID token - used as the authorization bearer <token> for other endpoints.

refresh_token.description: Refresh token - used for refreshing the bearer token at the Customer Refresh Token endpoint.

id.description: Also known as customerID when used in other endpoint requests.

post.description: Allows an applicant to sign in by providing valid credentials.

invite_token.description: Invitation token (optional, required if email and password are not provided)

post.description: Allows users (Admin, Customer, Applicant) to update their password.

new_password.description: The new password the user wants to set

schema.description: Both old_password and new_password are required to update the password.

post.description: Allows an admin to request a password reset link via email.

message.example: A link to reset your password has been sent to the registered email

example.message: A link to reset your password has been sent to the registered email

post.description: Validates the password reset token before allowing the user to reset their password.
Allows a user to reset their password using a valid reset token.

password.description: The new password to set after reset

reset_token.description: The token required to validate the password reset request

schema.description: Both password and reset_token are required for password reset.

message.example: This reset password link has expired

example.message: This reset password link has expired
